codeReview.summary: Core budget APIs/UI are implemented and broadly align with scope, but there are correctness gaps around department key normalization (causing budget/actual mismatches) and lifecycle cleanup for the new BudgetEnvelope model.

issues.description: Department aggregation uses raw employee department text after only checking trim truthiness (`emp.department` instead of `emp.department.trim()`). Because employee creation accepts untrimmed department strings (`packages/server/src/controllers/employeeController.ts:51`), entries like "Engineering " will not match envelope "Engineering", producing incorrect summary totals/status.

Client-side real-time summary repeats the same normalization bug (`emp.department` is used as map key when non-empty after trim). This causes immediate UI drift/misclassification for departments with leading/trailing whitespace, undermining the real-time budget impact requirement.
Scenario deletion cascades to Employee documents only; the new BudgetEnvelope model introduced by this feature is not cleaned up. This leaves orphaned envelopes tied to deleted scenarios.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Department normalization expectations are not clearly codified in shared state. Employee inputs allow untrimmed department values while budget aggregation assumes exact string matching, leading to mismatches.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/server/src/controllers/employeeController.ts:51 uses `z.string().min(1)` (no trim); packages/server/src/controllers/budgetController.ts:262 and packages/client/src/utils/budgetMetrics.ts:56 use raw department strings as grouping keys.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is no explicit shared rule for updating scenario lifecycle cleanup when introducing new scenario-scoped models, which likely contributed to missing BudgetEnvelope cascade deletion.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/server/src/controllers/scenarioController.ts:96 deletes Employee records on scenario delete, but no corresponding BudgetEnvelope cleanup exists.

summary: Result: fail. The feature is largely complete, but department-key normalization bugs in both server and client summaries can produce incorrect budget utilization/alerts, and BudgetEnvelope records are not cascaded on scenario deletion.
